verses.dev: उषित्वा रजनीं तत्र राजपुत्रावरिन्दमौ।महर्षिमभिवाद्याथ जग्मतुस्तं गिरिं प्रति
verses.trans: After spending the night, the princes, destroyers of enemies, offered obeisance to that great sage (Bharadhwaja) and went towards that mount Chitrkuta.

words.m: in the direction of that mountain

verses.dev: तेषां चैव स्वस्त्ययनं महर्षि स्स चकार ह।प्रस्थितांश्चैव तान्प्रेक्ष्य पिता पुत्रानिवान्वगात्
verses.trans: Seeing them setting forth, the great sage blessed them for a safe journey and followed them like a father following his children.

verses.dev: ततः प्रचक्रमे वक्तुं वचनं स महामुनिः।भरद्वाजो महातेजा रामं सत्यपराक्रमम्
verses.trans: Then that great effulgent sage Bharadwaja began to speak to Rama whose strength was truth.

words.m: to one whose strength is truth

verses.dev: गङ्गायमुनयो स्सन्धिमासाद्य मनुजर्षभौ।कालिन्दीमनुगच्छेतां नदीं पश्चान्मुखाश्रिताम्
verses.trans: Reaching the confluence of rivers Ganga and Yamuna, O Rama and Lakshmana best among men proceed along the Kalindi (Yamuna) river flowing westward.

words.m: the confluence of Ganga and Yamuna

verses.dev: अथाऽसाद्य तु कालिन्दी शीघ्रस्रोतसमापगाम्।तस्यास्तीर्थं प्रचलितं पुराणं प्रेक्ष्य राघवौतत्र यूयं प्लवं कृत्वा तरतांशुमतीं नदीम्।
verses.trans: On reaching that swiftflowing river Yamuna, O Rama and Lakshmana O scions of the Raghu race, you will find an ancient spot (on the river bank). There make a raft and cross Kalindi, daughter of the Sun.

words.m: the scions of the Raghus (Rama and Lakshmana)

verses.dev: ततो न्यग्रोधमासाद्य महान्तं हरितच्छदम्विवृद्धं बहुभिर्वृक्षै श्श्यामं सिद्धोपसेवितम्।तस्मै सीताञ्जलिं कृत्वा प्रयुञ्जीताशिषश्शिवाः
verses.trans: There you will find a large, banyan tree with its lush green leaves, looking dark with its densely grown trees. It is inhabited by siddhas. Sita should offer with folded palms invocations for her wellbeing.

words.m: inhabited by siddhas (sages who have attained perfection)

verses.dev: ततो न्यग्रोधमासाद्य महान्तं हरितच्छदम्विवृद्धं बहुभिर्वृक्षै श्श्यामं सिद्धोपसेवितम्।तस्मै सीताञ्जलिं कृत्वा प्रयुञ्जीताशिषश्शिवाः
verses.trans: There you will find a large, banyan tree with its lush green leaves, looking dark with its densely grown trees. It is inhabited by siddhas. Sita should offer with folded palms invocations for her wellbeing.

words.m: inhabited by siddhas (sages who have attained perfection)

verses.dev: समासाद्य तु तं वृक्षं वसेद्वातिक्रमेत वा।क्रोशमात्रं ततो गत्वा नीलं द्रक्ष्यथ काननम्पलाशबदरीमिश्रं रम्यं वंशैश्च यामुनैः।
verses.trans: Reaching that tree, you may tarry or proceed further. A krosa from there, you will see a beautiful forest filled with dark trees mixed with Palasas and badaris and bamboos on the Yamuna.

words.m: at a distance of a krosa
mixed with Palasa and Badari trees

verses.dev: स पन्थाश्चित्रकूटस्य गत स्सुबहुशो मयारम्यो मार्दवयुक्तश्च वनदावैर्विवर्जितः।
verses.trans: That path leads to Chitrakuta. I had travelled through it several times. It is beautiful, smooth and safe from forest fires.

verses.dev: इति पन्थानमावेद्य महर्षिस्सन्यवर्ततअभिवाद्य तथेत्युक्त्वा रामेण विनिवर्तितः।
verses.trans: The great sage returned after instructing Rama about the path. So be it, said Rama and offering obeisance requested Bharadhwaja to go back.

verses.dev: उपावृत्ते मुनौ तस्मिन्रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्कृतपुण्याः स्म सौमित्रे मुनिर्यन्नोऽनुकम्पते।
verses.trans: With the sage gone, Rama said, O Saumitri (son of Sumitra) we are lucky, for the sage has been so kind.

verses.dev: इति तौ पुरुषव्याघ्रौ मन्त्रयित्वा मनस्विनौ।सीतामेवाग्रतः कृत्वा कालिन्दीं जग्मतुर्नदीम्
verses.trans: Thus talking with one another, the two nobleminded tigers (best) among men advanced towards river Kalindi (Yamuna) with Sita walking in front.

verses.dev: अथाऽसाद्य तु कालिन्दीं शीघ्रस्रोतोवहां नदीम्।चिन्तामापेदिरे सर्वे नदीजलतितीर्षवः
verses.trans: Thereafter, they all reached the rapidly flowing river Kalindi and began pondering how to cross it.

verses.dev: तौ काष्ठसङ्घातमथो चक्रतु स्सुमहाप्लवम्।शुष्कैर्वंशै स्समास्तीर्णमुशीरैश्च समावृतम्
verses.trans: Then they both made a big wooden float by joining logs of wood, spreading bamboos on it and covering it with the (fragrant roots of) Ushira bushes.

words.m: with logs of wood joined together

verses.dev: ततो वेतसशाखाश्च जम्बूशाखाश्च वीर्यवान्।चकार लक्ष्मणश्छित्वा सीताया स्सुखमासनम्
verses.trans: The valiant Lakshmana cut the stems of reeds and branches of the Jambu tree to prepare a comfortable seat for Sita (on that float).

verses.dev: तत्र श्रियमिवाचिन्त्यां रामो दाशरथिः प्रियाम्। ईषत्संलज्जमानां तामध्यारोपयतप्लवम्
verses.trans: Rama, son of Dasaratha helped his beloved (Sita), who was looking inconceivably like Lakshmi (goddess of wealth) and was feeling a little bashful while stepping on to the float.

verses.dev: पार्श्वे च तत्र वैदेह्या वसने भूषणानि च।प्लवे कठिनकाजं च रामश्चक्रे सहायुधैः
verses.trans: Rama placed robes and ornaments on the float by the side of Sita and a spade and a basket along with weapons.

verses.dev: आरोप्य प्रथमं सीतां सङ्घाटं परिगृह्य तौ।तत प्रतेरतुर्यत्तौ वीरौ दशरथात्मजौ
verses.trans: Those valiant sons of Dasaratha held the float (firmly) to help Sita step on to it. Then they crossed the river carefully.

verses.dev: कालिन्दीमध्यमायाता सीता त्वेनामवन्दत।स्वस्ति देवि तरामि त्वां पारये न्मे पतिर्व्रतम्यक्ष्ये त्वां गोसहस्रेण सुराघटशतेन च।स्वस्ति प्रत्यागते रामे पुरी मिक्ष्वाकुपालिताम्
verses.trans: On reaching the middle of the river Yamuna, Sita made reverential salutations to her saying O goddess, I am crossing you, may safety be ours and may my husband fulfil his vow After Rama's (safe) return to the city (of Ayodhya) ruled by the Ikshvaku kings, I shall worship you with a thousand cows and a hundred pots of wine.

words.m: the middle of the river Kalindi (Yamuna)
with the offer of a thousand cows
with a hundred pots of wine

verses.dev: कालिन्दीमध्यमायाता सीता त्वेनामवन्दत।स्वस्ति देवि तरामि त्वां पारये न्मे पतिर्व्रतम्यक्ष्ये त्वां गोसहस्रेण सुराघटशतेन च।स्वस्ति प्रत्यागते रामे पुरी मिक्ष्वाकुपालिताम्
verses.trans: On reaching the middle of the river Yamuna, Sita made reverential salutations to her saying O goddess, I am crossing you, may safety be ours and may my husband fulfil his vow After Rama's (safe) return to the city (of Ayodhya) ruled by the Ikshvaku kings, I shall worship you with a thousand cows and a hundred pots of wine.

words.m: the middle of the river Kalindi (Yamuna)
with the offer of a thousand cows
with a hundred pots of wine
